hero.headline: Shoolini Online MBA in Operation and Supply Chain Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Pay After Placement and NAAC A+ accreditation

sections.tldr: Shoolini Online MBA in Operation and Supply Chain Management covers procurement, warehouse management, logistics, lean operations, demand planning, and ERP systems. PAP option Rs 1,50,000 or opt-out Rs 1,30,000. LinkedIn Learning access. KPMG certifications. NAAC A+. NIRF #69 University.

about.body:
Operations and Supply Chain Management is a high-demand MBA specialisation driven by e-commerce growth, manufacturing sector expansion, and the formalisation of India's logistics sector under GST. Operations managers and supply chain professionals are required across FMCG, auto ancillary, pharmaceutical, e-commerce, and government infrastructure sectors.

Shoolini University Online's MBA in Operation and Supply Chain Management covers production operations management, procurement and vendor management, warehouse and inventory management, logistics and transportation, lean manufacturing and Six Sigma basics, ERP systems, and demand planning and forecasting. The programme is designed for professionals in operations, logistics, or manufacturing who want to move from functional roles into supply chain management.

Shoolini University holds NIRF #69 (University, 2025) and NAAC A+ accreditation. The Digital and Technological Solutions subject in the Sem 1 core builds the ERP and technology literacy foundation that is increasingly mandatory for supply chain management professionals.

The Pay After Placement option on Track 1 is available. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are common core for all Shoolini MBA specialisations. Sem 3-4 specialisation-specific subjects are provided by Shoolini at the admission stage. Contact our counsellor for the full Operations and Supply Chain Sem 3-4 curriculum.

subjects.name: Marketing Management
subjects.desc: Core marketing principles, market segmentation, consumer decision models, and B2B and B2C strategy fundamentals.

subjects.name: Organizational Behaviour
subjects.desc: Team dynamics, motivation theories, leadership styles, conflict resolution, and organisational culture frameworks.

subjects.name: Financial Accounting
subjects.desc: Preparation of financial statements, accounting standards, ratio analysis, and management reporting basics.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship
subjects.desc: Business model design, lean startup methodology, venture capital basics, and opportunity identification frameworks.

subjects.name: Creativity Decoded
subjects.desc: Creative problem-solving models, SCAMPER technique, lateral thinking, and innovation culture building.

subjects.name: Digital and Technological Solutions
subjects.desc: Digital transformation frameworks, cloud basics, ERP overview, and technology adoption in business management.

subjects.name: Functional English
subjects.desc: Business writing, email communication, report preparation, and professional oral communication skills.

subjects.name: Human Resource Management
subjects.desc: Recruitment and selection, training and development, performance management, and compensation design.

subjects.name: Financial Management
subjects.desc: Capital structure, working capital management, capital budgeting, and cost of capital analysis.

subjects.name: Career and Life Skills
subjects.desc: Goal setting, personal effectiveness, professional networking, and career planning frameworks.

subjects.name: Managerial Economics
subjects.desc: Demand analysis, pricing strategies, market structures, and business forecasting methods.

subjects.name: Statistics for Management
subjects.desc: Descriptive statistics, probability distributions, hypothesis testing, and regression for business.

subjects.name: Functional English - 2
subjects.desc: Advanced business writing, executive summary preparation, and formal professional correspondence.

subjects.name: Presentation and Charts
subjects.desc: Data visualisation tools, chart selection, slide design principles, and storytelling with data.

subjects.name: Fundamentals of Direct Selling
subjects.desc: Direct selling business models, DSA regulatory framework, ethics in direct sales, and network building.

whoHires.body:
Operations and Supply Chain MBA graduates from Shoolini University Online are hired across manufacturing, logistics, and e-commerce sectors:

**Industries:** FMCG, auto and auto ancillary, pharmaceuticals, e-commerce, logistics and 3PL, retail, infrastructure, food and agri processing

**Entry-level roles:** Supply Chain Analyst, Procurement Executive, Warehouse Supervisor, Logistics Coordinator, Operations Trainee Manager, Inventory Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Supply Chain Manager, Operations Manager, Procurement Manager, Warehouse Manager, Distribution Head, Production Planning Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Supply Chain, Head of Operations, Chief Supply Chain Officer, Director of Procurement, Regional Operations Head, COO (manufacturing)

**Top hiring organisations:** Maruti Suzuki, Hero MotoCorp, Bajaj Auto, HUL, ITC, Marico, Sun Pharma, Cipla, Delhivery, Blue Dart, Ecom Express, Flipkart supply chain, Amazon India logistics, Reliance Retail supply chain, and mid-market manufacturing companies across Punjab, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand industrial corridors

skills.body:
The Operations and Supply Chain specialisation builds skills that manufacturing, FMCG, and logistics employers test at operations manager and supply chain analyst levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Supply Chain Management: demand forecasting models, EOQ and safety stock calculation, S&OP process design, and supplier performance scorecards
- Warehouse Management: warehouse layout optimisation, pick-pack-ship process design, WMS basics, and inventory accuracy improvement
- Procurement: RFQ and tender process management, vendor evaluation matrix, contract terms, and category spend analysis
- Lean Operations: value stream mapping basics, 5S implementation, OEE measurement, and kaizen event facilitation

**Applied skills:**
- ERP Systems: SAP MM and PP module navigation basics, ERP data entry workflows, and report extraction for supply chain KPIs
- Logistics: freight mode selection (road, rail, air), 3PL vendor management, reverse logistics basics, and last-mile delivery metrics

**Soft skills:** Presenting supply chain performance metrics to operations and finance leadership, managing vendor escalations under supply disruption, coordinating cross-functional sales and operations planning, and communicating production delays and mitigation plans to commercial teams.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Shoolini Online MBA Operations and SCM against peer programmes:

**vs CU Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** CU at Rs 1,65,000 Tier 1 offers NIRF #19, PwC co-teaching, Harvard certificate, and a dedicated logistics and supply chain curriculum. CU is stronger for candidates targeting logistics consulting or BFSI supply chain financing. Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 opt-out is 21% cheaper with NAAC A+ and PAP — the better fit for candidates in North India manufacturing, FMCG, or e-commerce operations roles.

**vs LPU Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** LPU has NAAC A++ and strong campus placement in Punjab manufacturing sector. Fee approximately Rs 1,50,000-1,80,000. Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 is comparably or more affordably priced while adding the PAP option. For Himachal Pradesh and Chandigarh region manufacturing sector hiring, Shoolini's NIRF #69 regional brand is an advantage.

**vs Symbiosis Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** Symbiosis carries premium brand in logistics and supply chain from its Pune campus associations. Fee is typically higher. Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 with PAP is substantially cheaper — the right choice for operations professionals seeking credentials at accessible cost while maintaining NAAC A+ compliance.

fees.body:
The Shoolini University Online MBA in Operation and Supply Chain Management carries a regular fee of Rs 2,00,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). A Rs 500 one-time application fee applies. No registration fee, no exam fees, no project fees beyond the programme fee.

**Two offer tracks**:

**Offer 1 - MBA with Pay After Placement (PAP)**:
- Rs 50,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,50,000 (25% savings).
- Payment split: Rs 1,20,000 before placement (Rs 30,000 x 4 semesters) + Rs 30,000 payable after confirmed placement.

**Offer 2 - MBA Opt-out from PAP**:
- Rs 70,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,30,000 (35% savings).
- Payment: Rs 32,500 x 4 semesters.

**Merit scholarship (10% additional)**:
For candidates scoring 90% or above in Class 12 (UG entry) or 90% or above in UG degree. Non-combinable with PAP or opt-out tracks, stackable with lump-sum and annual discounts.

**Additional payment discounts (stackable with merit only)**:
- 10% off on full course fee with single lump-sum payment
- Additional 5% off on annual payment in full

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Shoolini University (not via external marketing partners or NBFCs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Shoolini each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Shoolini University MBA degree certificate with Operation and Supply Chain Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards. NIRF #69 University and NAAC A+ carry recognition across India's manufacturing, logistics, and e-commerce employer base.

DigiLocker verification is available. The degree satisfies management qualification requirements for operations manager and supply chain manager roles at FMCG, auto, pharmaceutical, and logistics companies specifying a recognised UGC-DEB entitled university MBA.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Shoolini University Online MBA Operations and SCM alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Harpreet S.
items.liked: Supply chain management and ERP content matched exactly what manufacturing employers test at manager level
items.disliked: Green supply chain and sustainability content was not covered — relevant for FMCG sector procurement roles
items.body: I work in procurement for an auto ancillary manufacturer in Chandigarh. The supply chain management, vendor evaluation, and ERP navigation subjects were directly applicable to my day-to-day work. PAP structure gave me flexibility to invest in the MBA without disrupting my family's cash flow. NAAC A+ from Shoolini cleared the management degree requirement for my company's Materials Manager promotion criteria. LinkedIn Learning SAP supply chain courses supplemented the academic content.

items.name: Sanjay M.
items.liked: Warehouse management and lean operations content gave practical frameworks for my 3PL company
items.disliked: More e-commerce fulfilment-specific content would be useful as the logistics sector shifts to last-mile
items.body: I manage operations at a 3PL company in Ludhiana. Warehouse layout optimisation, OEE measurement, and WMS basics subjects gave me frameworks I immediately applied to our DC operations. Statistics for Management helped me build delivery performance dashboards. KPMG certification added credibility for enterprise client conversations. The Rs 1,30,000 opt-out fee at NAAC A+ from a NIRF #69 university was excellent value.

items.name: Poonam K.
items.liked: S&OP process and demand forecasting subjects were immediately applicable to my FMCG planning role
items.disliked: Supply chain finance and reverse logistics content needed more India-specific examples
items.body: I work in demand planning at an FMCG company. The S&OP process design and demand forecasting subjects matched exactly what I do, and gave me structured frameworks to replace the informal processes we had been using. PAP track was psychologically reassuring even though I already had employment. NIRF #69 and NAAC A+ from Shoolini satisfied my company's degree requirement for the Supply Chain Manager promotion.

faqs.question: What ERP and technology content is covered in Operations and SCM?
faqs.answer: The Sem 1 Digital and Technological Solutions subject covers ERP overview, technology adoption in business, and digital tools for operations management. The specialisation Sem 3-4 subjects deepen into SAP MM and PP module navigation, WMS basics, and supply chain technology platforms. LinkedIn Learning access provides additional courses in SAP, Oracle SCM, and supply chain analytics tools.

faqs.question: Is Shoolini Operations MBA valid for manufacturing and logistics sector manager roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Shoolini MBA is UGC-DEB entitled and NAAC A+ accredited, satisfying management degree requirements at manufacturing, FMCG, pharmaceutical, and logistics companies specifying a recognised university MBA for operations and supply chain manager roles. NIRF #69 University ranking adds credibility for both private sector and PSU employer filters.

faqs.question: What lean manufacturing content is covered?
faqs.answer: The Operations and SCM specialisation covers lean manufacturing principles including value stream mapping, 5S implementation, OEE measurement, kaizen facilitation, and waste elimination methodologies. These are directly applicable for candidates in manufacturing, auto ancillary, and process industry operations roles targeting lean or continuous improvement functions.

faqs.question: How does PAP work for Operations and SCM candidates?
faqs.answer: PAP applies to all 16 Shoolini MBA specialisations. Under PAP, candidates pay Rs 1,20,000 before placement (Rs 30,000 per semester) and Rs 30,000 after confirmed placement. For operations professionals changing jobs or industries, PAP aligns the fee deferred component with employment confirmation rather than requiring full payment before the career transition is secured.

faqs.question: How does Shoolini Operations MBA compare to CU and LPU in this space?
faqs.answer: Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 opt-out is 21% cheaper than CU Tier 1 (Rs 1,65,000) and comparably priced to LPU. CU adds PwC certification and Harvard certificate for candidates seeking consulting or corporate advisory-oriented credentials. Shoolini adds PAP and Adda247. For manufacturing and logistics professionals in North India, Shoolini's regional brand and fee structure are competitive.

chooseIf: You work in operations, supply chain, logistics, or manufacturing and want a NAAC A+ MBA with Pay After Placement.
Your budget is Rs 1,30,000 with KPMG certifications and LinkedIn Learning bundled.
You are in North India manufacturing, FMCG, or e-commerce logistics targeting a formal management credential.
You want India's only online MBA with Pay After Placement for an operations and supply chain career.
consider: You want PwC consulting-grade content and Harvard certificate for logistics advisory roles (consider CU).
You need Symbiosis brand specifically for Pune manufacturing corridor employer recognition.
You need NAAC A++ and LPU's Punjab campus placement network (consider LPU).
